Feature-length episode starring Martin Shaw as the 1960s Northumberland detective, with Lee Ingleby and Robert Glenister(n)

A potential witness is then found dead, and Brian and Jack are seconded to the unit handling the case, only to find Emily Driscoll (Dennis Waterman's daughter Hannah) is heading the operation. programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Couple Nicole and Lewis compete in the culinary contest, teaming up with chefs Mike Reid and Akis Petretzikis. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Presented by Alex Jones and Matt Baker(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers compete to design a zen space for a young couple longing for a Japanese-style garden. Working to a £7,000 budget, the designers attempt to bring a taste of the Orient to Blackburn, turning a mismatched garden into an area of tranquillity and serenity(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce chairs the topical debate from Shrewsbury, where the panel discuss the coronavirus and the implications that are affecting the UK and the rest of the world(n)

programme.title: Noughts + Crosses
programme.desc: Albion is reeling from the aftermath of events at Demwa Hospital, but Sephy has just one question on her mind - how did Callum know what was going to happen? As soon as Ryan discovers what has happened, he gives himself up to the police and confesses in order to save his sons. Meggie is horrified to discover what has happened to her family, and Jasmine offers to pay for Ryan's legal defence costs. Drama, starring Masali Baduza, Jack Rowan, Helen Baxendale and Paterson Joseph(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Part two of two. Danny takes Gerry on the run as they search for the proof they need to prove his innocence - despite Gerry remaining tight-lipped about his involvement with dodgy dealings in his old unit. The stakes are high as the Unsolved Crime and Open Case Squad goes after one of the biggest crime families in London, and it's those closest to Gerry who are dangerously put in the firing line. Starring Dennis Waterman, Nicholas Lyndhurst, Tamzin Outhwaite and Denis Lawson(n)

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Fergus Walsh present the latest on the coronavirus pandemic(n)

programme.title: London Kills
programme.desc: After a woman's body is washed up from the Thames, the detectives believe the key to the crime is a sleazy dating website. Crime drama, starring Hugo Speer and Sharon Small(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: BBC News at 9
programme.desc: The latest on the coronavirus pandemic, focusing on the latest measure taken in the UK and around the world(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of property developments in Plymouth, Treherbert in Wales and Hinckley in Leicestershire that all turn out to take much longer than planned, threatening to prevent their new owners making money(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: When flooding hits a business, the owner goes to extraordinary lengths to try to rubber stamp her claim. A dog owner displays very bad behaviour when it comes to their pet's insurance policy, while there's a backlash over a whiplash case when footage emerges of the claimant in very fine health indeed. Narrated by Naga Munchetty(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams at the Peterborough Festival of Antiques find themselves in the capable hands of experts Stephanie Connell and Philip Serrell, trying to bag items they can make a profit on at auction. As well as monitoring proceedings, presenter Eric Knowles has an intriguing mystery to solve(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Julia supports Jimmi as he struggles with life after prison. Later, she unexpectedly offers him a way out of Letherbridge, but whether he will accept it remains to be seen(n)

programme.title: London Kills
programme.desc: A manipulative murderer strikes too close to home for the squad's comfort. With Billie stabbed and her attacker on the loose, Vivienne blames herself for not recognising that she was in danger. As the team scrambles to track down Amber, they discover that there's more to her than meets the eye. Detective drama, starring Bailey Patrick, Tori Allen-Martin, Sharon Small and Hugo Speer(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple fulfil a long-held dream of moving to the Scottish countryside of Ayrshire. Alongside a selection of the region's best properties that a budget of £450,000 can buy, Nicki also pays a visit to the 18th-century Dumfries House, home to one of the finest collections of furniture in the world(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore searches for tired and unwanted items at Woking Recycling Centre in Surrey she can transform from skip-bound to stylish. With three items saved, Sarah turns to upholsterer Sharon O'Connor to overhaul an old chair frame, while upcycler Jacqui Joseph attempts to transform a dated desk. Sarah's challenge is to update a set of four chairs. Narrated by Arthur Smith(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Siblings from Wolverhampton are paired with chefs Ellis Barrie and Romy Gill. Sommer and Daryl's large family gatherings revolve around shared meals and they were both taught to cook by their grandmother, who cheers them on from the audience. Fiercely competitive, Sommer intends to beat her little brother, but Daryl is determined he can get the better of his sister. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Ex-Manchester United defender Gary Pallister, former WBC World Super-Middleweight champion Richie Woodhall, Olympic gold medal-winning hockey player Jane Sixsmith, and former sprinter Tony Jarrett join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Gary decides to join the dating scene so he can bring someone along to Stevie's birthday party, prompting Miranda to do the same. Are the two of them destined to find romance in the arms of strangers - or with each other? Meanwhile, the shop owner can't hide her embarrassment when Penny decides to run for local councillor. Comedy, starring Miranda Hart, Tom Ellis and Patricia Hodge(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Actress Olivia Colman, JLS member Aston Merrygold, comedian Jon Richardson and wildlife presenter Michaela Strachan join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Six heat winners return for the quarter-final, fighting for a place in Knockout Week. Facing the Critic's Test, the week's best-performing amateurs take on an intimidating task - cooking to a brief set by food writer Jimi Famurewa, who asks for a dish inspired by flavours of the sweet shop. Presented by John Torode and Gregg Wallace(n)

programme.title: Peter Kay's Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: A further collection of clips documenting the much-loved comedy star's best work from his decades in showbusiness. Featuring a special appearance by The One Show's Alex Jones(n)

programme.title: Witness (1985)
programme.desc: A Philadelphia cop is assigned to protect an Amish boy who was the only witness to a murder. Discovering the killers are rogue policemen willing to go to any lengths to cover up their crime, he is forced to go into hiding within the Amish community, experiencing a clash of cultures and unexpected feelings for the boy's widowed mother. Thriller, directed by Peter Weir, with Harrison Ford, Kelly McGillis, Lukas Haas and Danny Glover(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Flog It! Trade Secrets
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the show's experts return to offer more advice on making money from antiques and collectibles. In the first edition, they focus on items that sparkle when they catch the light - including an unusual adornment for a dog, and a pair of space age compacts. Plus, Will Axon tries his hand at medal making on a visit to the Royal Mint, and a fan of the show reveals how she sold a collection of hat pins to indulge in her love of vintage glamour(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Chefs Ellis Barrie and Mike Reid create a meal in 20 minutes, using ingredients brought on to the show by two co-workers who grow their own vegetables. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's journey through eastern England takes him to the Snape Maltings music and arts venue, the Norfolk seaside resort of Great Yarmouth and the city of Norwich(n)

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Lindisfarne, which lies off the Northumberland coast. From what became known as the Holy Island of Lindisfarne, monks spread Christianity to the rest of northern England and created one of Britain's finest treasures - the Lindisfarne Gospels. Viking attacks caused the monks to flee but the village survived and today the community has to share it with thousands of tourists(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Nick Crane follows the Welsh coast path around Anglesey, presenting tales of inspired thinking and coastal ingenuity. Tess Dunlop reveals how a small French fishing village gave rise to the birth of British commercial radio in the 1930s, Brendan Walker investigates why the 1970 collapse of Pembrokeshire's Cleddau Bridge changed the engineering world for ever, Dick Strawbridge is in Portsmouth, where he tells of a novel First World War plan to disguise British ships by hiding them in plain sight(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Mark Stacey face off at an auction in Sevenoaks, where Christina plays catch-up and Mark falls in love with a romantic painting(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Mason McQueen's black cab takes Terry Wogan to the Victorian spa town of Harrogate in North Yorkshire, where they enjoy a Turkish bath and drink inordinate amounts of tea. They also sample a toffee that is actually a barley sugar, visit Agatha Christie's hide-out hotel and enjoy a slap-up dinner - with Yorkshire pudding for desert(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Johno lashes out after taking Rachel's case too personally. Meanwhile, Patricia decides whether to persist with her sordid scheme, and the Kielys are divided over their next move(n)

programme.title: Alex Polizzi: Chefs on Trial
programme.desc: MasterChef meets The Apprentice as Alex Polizzi takes on the challenge of finding new head chefs for restaurants that have a desperate need to fill a vacancy. Over four nights, she offers up a total of nine candidates who are put through a rigorous interview and testing process, with only the very best making it through to the final. She begins her search at The Gilpin in the Lake District, a fine dining establishment whose family owners want to find a new executive head chef who will care as much about the business as they do(n)

programme.title: The TV That Made Me
programme.desc: Former Blue Peter presenter and intrepid adventurer Helen Skelton joins Brian Conley for a trip down memory lane, revisiting the classic archive TV that helped shape her into the person she now is. Clips include excerpts of Newsround, the early antics of Geordie duo Ant and Dec, the extreme challenges of The Krypton Factor and children's favourite Pingu(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Culzean Castle on the west coast of Scotland in Ayrshire, experts Anita Manning and Natasha Raskin Sharp are on hand to find a selection of objects to take to auction. A watercolour with a local connection catches Anita's eye, while Natasha is drawn to a gorgeous handwoven blanket. Paul Martin explores a hidden part of Culzean's history, uncovering the tales of smugglers working the coastline in the caves beneath the castle walls(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Presenter Kelly Cates, actor Tyger Drew-Honey, comedian Desiree Burch and journalist Matt Allwright take part in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Newport, where he learns about the heroism of a young boy who was awarded the Albert Medal, before visiting a cinema dating from 1912 in Clevedon(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A volunteer in Scotland has to rescue his own father, whose fishing boat is drifting powerless towards a rocky shoreline. In Bristol, a father and son are among the crew that comes to the aid of two five-year-old girls who have drifted into the Bristol channel on an inflatable, while in Ilfracombe, lifeboat volunteers race against time to save a mother and her daughter stranded at the base of huge sea cliffs(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton sets sail through the Kyles of Bute to the Holy Loch and on to Ardentinny, his childhood home overlooking Loch Long. He also discovers a Viking connection in the shape of a vintage sailing boat that takes him to scale the heights of the people's mountain - The Cobbler(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: As the competition continues, chefs Kerth Gumbs, Steve Groves, Karl O'Dell and Ivan Tisdall-Downes compete in the heats for the London and South East region. Each chef prepares a starter and fish course celebrating iconic food from children's literature, including a dish layering textures of carrot and goats cheese inspired by Beatrix Potter, and a hazelnut dish celebrating author Julia Donaldson's acclaimed picture book, The Gruffalo. Presented by Susan Calman(n)

programme.title: Growing Up Gifted
programme.desc: Part one of two. Return of the documentary following three gifted teenagers, Liam, Kian and Jamarley, as they prepare to finish secondary school and make important decisions about their futures. Kian dreams of becoming a theoretical physicist, but has to face up to the reality of having to leave Hartlepool to pursue his dreams. In Newcastle, aspiring doctor Liam finds his work ethic is increasingly under scrutiny, while in London, Jamarley faces pressures outside school(n)

programme.title: Mister Winner
programme.desc: Comedy following the misadventures of Leslie Winner, an eternally-optimistic yet calamitous character, as he prepares for married life. The pressure is on Leslie to find - and keep - a job to pay for his honeymoon and wedding, and to impress his disapproving father-in-law. In the opening episode, Leslie comes across an abandoned piano and comes up with a clever money-making scheme to help pay for his upcoming wedding. Starring Spencer Jones, Lucy Pearman and Shaun Williamson(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, Touker Suleyman, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani and Peter Jones cast their eyes over the final batch of business pitches of the current series. Stepping into the arena are an inventor who has come up with an unusual way of controlling household draughts, a businessman who claims there is money to be made in encouraging dogs to eat bugs, a fitness teacher who launches into a shimmying session, and the creator of a high-end garment bag. Evan Davis oversees proceedings as the Dragons decide if any of these pitches are worthy of investment(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: Five pairs of competitors journey around the globe, carrying only limited cash and unable to get on a plane or use mobile phones and credit cards. In the first edition, the teams set off from Mexico City, hoping to reach the first of seven checkpoints - Copan Ruinas in Honduras. Each team adopts a different strategy, with married couple Jen and Rob quickly deciding on a route via Belize, but confusion on the Metro leads to a delay(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on the work of staff at Alder Hey Children's Hospital. Eight-month-old Elsie was not expected to survive being born due to serious kidney abnormalities. But she has spent all but two weeks of her life in hospital and has 11 operations to repair the damage caused by a genetic problem. Now she faces surgery yet again. Holly has cerebral palsy and, as she is about to turn 18, is preparing to transition to adult services in Liverpool - a huge leap for her parents who have grown to depend on the hospital's care(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: Footage shows the moment a teenager fell from a waterfall, and on a busy dual carriageway, motorists attempt to rescue a man who has passed out at the wheel of his van(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Flog It! Trade Secrets
programme.desc: The experts recall the moments they made their debuts on the show, while Claire Rawle explores the life and work of designer Christopher Dresser.(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Mother and son duo Pat and Tommy compete in the culinary contest, teaming up with chefs Romy Gill and Ellis Barrie. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from the grounds of Salisbury Cathedral in Wiltshire, where the experts value decorative objects and rarities belonging to the public. An eclectic mix includes one of Napoleon's razors, letters from Enid Blyton and an item of Queen Victoria's underwear, as well as a hand-painted Christmas card from the Second World War and original drawings of Judge Dredd(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team investigates the culture, history and legends of Britain and its people, beginning with the nation's islands. Nick Crane learns how Fair Isle's small community has survived and Neil Oliver visits Orkney to explore conspiracy theories surrounding the death of Lord Kitchener. Tessa Dunlop heads for the Western Isles to investigate an optical phenomenon known as the green ray, Andy Torbet climbs a chalk pinnacle on the Isle of Wight, and folk singer June Tabor performs a traditional ballad(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Mark Stacey continue their rivalry at Newark Antique Fair, where he buys some kitsch poodles while she chooses a silver piggy(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Broadcaster Terry Wogan and London cabbie Mason McQueen visit Winchester, where they tuck into bangers at a local pub and sushi at a Wasabi Farm. They also meet an artist who is farming water buffalo, are given bread and beer at the Hospital of St Cross, and Mason has a go at fly-fishing for the first time and catches a trout(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Terry Wogan and Mason McQueen arrive in Birmingham on the latest leg of their culinary journey. They take in the sights and sounds of the Bull Ring market, where they feast on the finest dim sum, take a canal trip through the heart of the city - leading to an impromptu ballet lesson for Mason - and head to the famous Balti Triangle, where they help to create a special Irish curry(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: The station is shaken by the return of a familiar face, Keith's impulsive act has dire consequences, and Ash is shocked by where the ring evidence leads(n)

programme.title: Alex Polizzi: Chefs on Trial
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi continues her mission to find an executive head chef for Lake District restaurant The Gilpin, testing three new candidates' butchery skills as they are presented with a traditional saddle of hogget. Italian chef Michael surprises the family with his unusual approach, but will it be enough to beat Hrishikesh's East-West fusion approach to fine cuisine? Either way, only one can be selected to go through to Thursday's final(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Nick Hall assess items brought to Boconnoc House in Cornwall, while presenter Paul martin participates in a traditional Cornish gig race(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Presenter Kelly Cates, actor Tyger Drew-Honey, comedian Desiree Burch and journalist Matt Allwright continue the daily test of their skills, hosted by Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels between Taunton and Newton Abbot. Along the way he joins in with a fertility dance in Stogumber, visits a celebrated garden at Hestercombe to discover the fruits of an unusual partnership, and enjoys a trip aboard the longest heritage line in England(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the unpaid volunteers who sacrifice their free time to staff more than two hundred RNLI lifeboat stations across the UK(n)

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Walberswick, the former medieval port on the Suffolk coast, which is now a favourite retreat for writers, artists and day trippers. For centuries the village lurched from prosperity to poverty and in medieval times a bitter battle for supremacy was fought against the neighbouring port of Dunwich(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The remaining three London and South East chefs try to impress as they serve up their main courses and desserts inspired by children's literature, with one of the contestants meet War Horse author Michael Morpurgo. Veteran judge Tommy Banks then blind tastes and rank their mini-puddings, before the dessert proper sees dishes inspired by One Snowy Night, The Faraway Tree and Winnie the Pooh(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The documentary looks at Alder Hey Children's Hospital's craniofacial department - one of only four in the UK that treats life-threatening congenital skull and facial abnormalities. With a rise in referrals, the department is over-subscribed and neurosurgeon Chris Parks, who leads the craniofacial surgical team, faces a non-stop workload. Chris always works hand in glove with Ben Robertson, one of three paediatric maxillofacial surgeons in the UK. But this partnership is about to be broken up, as Ben is leaving to return to his native Australia. It takes 23 years' training to become a maxillofacial Consultant specialising in cranio-surgery, and there is currently nobody experienced enough to replace Ben, putting extra pressure on the waiting lists(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Adam Hills, host of Channel 4's The Last Leg, is the latest master of ceremonies at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He introduces US comic Michelle Wolf and sharp-witted Jamali Maddix to the stage(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Lesley Sharp narrates the story of a landmark legal battle by a group of women aiming to uncover the truth about birth defects in the Northamptonshire town being caused by toxic waste from the steelworks that had not been disposed of safely. The case had worldwide legal and medical ramifications as it was the first time that a court had recognised a link between airborne toxins and foetal development damage(n)

programme.title: Shane Williams: Rugby, Concussion and Me
programme.desc: Shane Williams heads on a journey to understand how the world of rugby, from grass roots to the highest levels, is working to reduce concussions in the modern-day game and helping to make the sport safer for future generations. He talks to players past and present, the custodians of the game at World Rugby and leading global medical professionals studying the long-term effects of contact sport on the brain(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Six contestants cook their Signature Dish and after tasting all plates, John Torode and Gregg Wallace pick their two favourites and those cooks earn themselves a MasterChef apron and place in the next round. The remaining four take the Three Ingredients Challenge, where they are tasked with creating one exceptional dish showcasing three specific ingredients - apple, almonds and vanilla. After that there is one more challenge standing in their way from a quarter-final place. Here, the contestants' fate is in the hands of former participants Alex Rushmer, Dr Tim Kinnaird and Dhruv Baker(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The second quarter-final sees the six talented heat winners return to fight for a place in Knockout Week. Facing the Critic's Test, the week's best-performing amateurs take on an intimidating task, cooking to a brief set by columnist, broadcaster and author Grace Dent, who asks them to produce a party-sharing plate. Hosted by John Torode and Gregg Wallace and narrated by India Fisher(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the Agricultural Showground on Anglesey, where antique experts Roo Irvine and Richard Madley guide two teams as they search for items of interest and value to take to auction. Charlie also travels back in time to explore crime and punishment at Beaumaris Gaol(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Couple Nicole and Lewis compete in the culinary contest, teaming up with chefs Mike Reid and Akis Petretzikis. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: Billy Bragg pays the Arderns a visit as they get to grips with 1960s life. The new decade brings a dizzying number of new developments, including skiffle music, hula hoops and sliced bread. It also brought about a seismic change in shopping with the introduction of self-service. The rise of supermarkets sees the personal service of the Arderns' shop under threat, forcing them to diversify their stock. The 1960s saw Sheffield becoming home to new workers from across the Commonwealth, many of whom share their memories of the era and the importance of the local corner shop to shift workers(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: An exploration of the history and culture of some of the most extreme locations in Britain. Nick Crane visits the Isles of Scilly, Ruth Goodman walks the dangerous path traversed daily by the Branscombe Cliff farmers in Devon, and Mark Horton investigates the Transatlantic Telegraph service. Meanwhile, Hermione Cockburn learns how the fossil of a large sea creature found at St David's Head, Pembrokeshire, shaped scientists' understanding of Earth's history(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: The week-long battle between antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Mark Stacey nears its end, but which of the pair will triumph is not yet clear. On the final day, the duo fight it out across four different locations, before racing towards the finish line at the Showdown Auction in Essex(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: London cabbie Mason McQueen and Terry Wogan head to Marlow, where they are intrigued to discover the historic Buckinghamshire town boasts many food producers and restaurants. The duo are served up a dish by Michelin-starred chef Atul Kochhar, treated to a pub lunch by Tom Kerridge and after Mason has a go at rowing on the River Thames, it's time to sample the county's regional dish of Bacon Badger(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Professional and personal pressures push Superintendent Dunne to breaking point, and Ash is appalled to see where her suspicions have led(n)

programme.title: Alex Polizzi: Chefs on Trial
programme.desc: The mission to find an executive head chef for Lake District restaurant The Gilpin continues. The last three candidates prepare a partridge fit for a fine-dining menu - and with only one place left in tomorrow's final, the pressure is on. While one of the chefs sets out to create something deserving of a Michelin star, another has trouble convincing the family he really wants the position, when it turns out he never stays in one job very long(n)

programme.title: The TV That Made Me
programme.desc: Gok Wan joins Brian Conley to revisit some classic television series of his formative years. From the magic and mystery of Japanese children's drama Monkey, to the pan-European quiz show Going for Gold, via groundbreaking, anarchic pop series The Tube, these were the shows that apparently helped shape Gok into the style icon that he is today(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Oxburgh Hall in Norfolk, Anita Manning discovers a Victorian dynamometer used for measuring the pressure of steam and David Harper is taken back to the days of ocean liners with a wine stopper from 1912. Presenter Paul Martin visits Cromer to find out about the RNLI's most decorated lifeboatman(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: The week's winner is crowned, as presenter Kelly Cates, actor Tyger Drew-Honey, comedian Desiree Burch and journalist Matt Allwright take on a series of challenging quiz games(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the trail of an historic achievement made at the dawn of the Edwardian era, Michael Portillo investigates the first radio signal to be sent across the Atlantic. Then, in Plymouth, he uncovers what happened to surviving crew members of the most famous ocean liner in history, the Titanic. Later, at Fowey, the former politician rediscovers a lost literary figure known as Q, who immortalised the town in his novels(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef explores Burgundy and the Auvergne, sampling a sorrel soup made by a chateau owner from the produce of the garden and showcasing Salers beef and Cantal cheese. Back in his Provencal country kitchen, Rick prepares Cantal Bourriols - buckwheat pancakes made with farmhouse eggs and Cep mushrooms(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Host Susan Calman invites the two highest-scoring London and the South-east chefs to go head to head and cook their six-course menus again. The duo need to impress formidable judges Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton and Andi Oliver as well as guest judge, the one-time Blue Peter presenter Konnie Huq, whose Cookie and the Most Annoying Boy in the World adventure series is making waves with young readers(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: The programme celebrates the humble snowdrop on a visit to one of the world's largest collections, lovingly nurtured in a suburban back garden, as Monty Don offers tips on what to do once they have finished flowering. At Longmeadow it's time to get dahlias into growth and, for inspiration, there's camera footage of a field full of dahlias in Sussex along with tips from a professional grower. Plus, Nick Bailey revels in the beauty of magnolias and Monty highlights the jobs to be getting on with this weekend(n)

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Istanbul
programme.desc: Journalist Adrian Chiles, former politician Edwina Currie, Olympian Fatima Whitbread, broadcaster Mim Shaikh, TV presenter Amar Latif, comedian Dom Joly and actress Pauline McLynn set out to cover sections of the Sultans Trail - a modern-day 2,200km pilgrimage across Eastern Europe that starts in Vienna and ends in Istanbul. Each has varying religions, with Dom and Pauline confirmed atheists. Starting in Serbia's capital Belgrade, they stay in basic hostels and sleep in shared dorms, acting as simple pilgrims traversing the ancient military route by rail and foot(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sally Phillips, Ross Noble and Nish Kumar join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig for an unconventional look at the theme 'Odorous and Odious'. During the course of the show, the panellists learn why the world's second-smelliest man is so frustrated. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window, as the fiendishly difficult questions lead to points being awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Mark Urban(n)

programme.title: The Lost City of Z (2016)
programme.desc: Drama telling the true story of British explorer Col Percy Fawcett, who in the 1920s told his fellows at the Royal Geographical Society in London he had found evidence of a secret city hidden away in the Amazon. But proving his theory meant returning yet again. Charlie Hunnam, Robert Pattinson and Sienna Miller star(n)

programme.title: BBC World News
programme.desc: The latest international headlines(n)

programme.title: Handmade on the Silk Road
programme.desc: The documentary series follows potter Abdol Reza Aghaei and his father in Meybod, southern Iran, as they make a simple decorated water jug, offering a portrait of two master craftsmen at work. Competing with cheap Chinese imports, the pair sometimes struggle to make a living, but are dedicated to keeping their traditions alive(n)

programme.title: The Victorian House of Arts and Crafts
programme.desc: The crafters are tasked to concentrate on the communal areas of the house, having to make from scratch a heavy metal weather vane, a decorative mirror, write, publish and print their own arts and crafts magazine and create a decorative pergola for the front of the house. It remains to be seen whether they can get all the jobs done in a week, just in time to celebrate with a ball and fireworks display. Later, presenters Anita Rani and Keith Brymer Jones assess what the participants have learned about life as a creative crafter in the Victorian era(n)

programme.title: Harry Birrell Presents Films of Love & War (2019)
programme.desc: The life of Harry Birrell, who recorded everything with a wonderfully cinematic eye on thousands of feet of high-quality 16mm film. From commanding a battalion of Gurkhas in the Indian army at the start of the Second World War, to dangerous sorties deep behind enemy lines in Burma at its end, and from the ballroom dances of his youth in the 1930s to teaching his children how to dance the twist in the 1960s, Harry's entertaining and errant adventures are filmed with the intimacy of home movies, but on the scale of Lawrence of Arabia. Narrated by Richard Madden(n)

programme.title: OJ: Made in America
programme.desc: OJ Simpson's trial for the murders of his ex-wife Nicole and her friend Ron Goldman, an event that became a media phenomenon during which there would be dozens of dramatic twists and turns, revelations and surprises, accusations and betrayals(n)

programme.title: OJ: Made in America
programme.desc: Documentary about the rise and fall of OJ Simpson, concluding with America's reaction to the verdict, and OJ's attempt to begin the next phase of his life(n)

programme.title: Sword, Musket & Machine Gun: Britain's Armed History
programme.desc: Sam Willis charts the impact of gunpowder on the battlefield, from cannons to the first hand-held weapons, and finds out what it is like to fire an English Civil War musket(n)

programme.title: The Victorian House of Arts and Crafts
programme.desc: The crafters are tasked to concentrate on the communal areas of the house, having to make from scratch a heavy metal weather vane, a decorative mirror, write, publish and print their own arts and crafts magazine and create a decorative pergola for the front of the house. It remains to be seen whether they can get all the jobs done in a week, just in time to celebrate with a ball and fireworks display. Later, presenters Anita Rani and Keith Brymer Jones assess what the participants have learned about life as a creative crafter in the Victorian era(n)

programme.title: BBC World News
programme.desc: The latest international headlines(n)

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham examines new scientific research that shows how subtle adaptations in the anatomy and physiology of big cats can contribute to the success of all stages of a hunt. He looks at how leg hairs help leopards stalk their prey, and how an enlarged area of lionesses' brains give them the edge(n)

programme.title: Shipwrecks: Britain's Sunken History
programme.desc: Maritime historian Dr Sam Willis travels to Africa to trace the origins and development of the women and children first protocol when abandoning ship - a code of conduct that came about in the Victorian era. He also reveals how the period oversaw trials of several other proposed solutions to high mortality rates at sea, such as life jackets, lifeboats, and rockets that could fire rescue lines on board stricken vessels(n)

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: Mark looks at some of the best films about British history in the order of when they are set, tracing the story of Britain from the Roman invasion to the modern era, via medieval times, the Tudors, the Civil War and the 18th and 19th centuries(n)

programme.title: Cromwell (1970)
programme.desc: The rise of Oliver Cromwell to power during the turbulent years of the mid-17th century, charting his days as an active parliamentarian to his victory over the royal armies in the English Civil War, and his subsequent role as the country's Lord Protector. Historical drama, with Richard Harris, Alec Guinness, Robert Morley, Dorothy Tutin, Frank Finlay, Patrick Magee, Charles Gray and Timothy Dalton(n)

programme.title: An Art Lovers' Guide
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez and Alastair Sooke explore the capital of Azerbaijan, which offers a mix of the ancient and modern in a country where cultural life is tightly controlled(n)

programme.title: Our Classical Century
programme.desc: Suzy Klein and Joan Bakewell explore classical music between 1953 and 1971, including the first broadcast of the Last Night Of The Proms and Britten's controversial War Requiem. The programme also examines the influence of Jacqueline du Pré, Daniel Barenboim, Yehudi Menuhin, and Ravi Shankar, and reveals how classical music became increasingly entangled with the more modern concepts of rock music, television and film(n)

programme.title: Shipwrecks: Britain's Sunken History
programme.desc: Maritime historian Dr Sam Willis travels to Africa to trace the origins and development of the women and children first protocol when abandoning ship - a code of conduct that came about in the Victorian era. He also reveals how the period oversaw trials of several other proposed solutions to high mortality rates at sea, such as life jackets, lifeboats, and rockets that could fire rescue lines on board stricken vessels(n)

programme.title: Kings of Soul
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating the male artists whose vocal stylings have played an instrumental role in the soul genre from the 60s to the 00s. Featuring footage of Brenton Wood performing Gimme Little Sign and Curtis Mayfield singing Keep On Keeping On, as well as appearances by Billy Preston, Bill Withers, Billy Ocean, Alexander O'Neal, Bobby Womack and Barry White(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first shown on June 8, featuring performances by the Beautiful South, Guns N' Roses and Jason Donovan(n)

programme.title: Rock 'n' Roll Island: Where Legends Were Born
programme.desc: The history of Eel Pie Island, a small island in the Thames in south-west London, which became the epicentre of R'n'B in the 1960s. In its heyday, the likes of the Rolling Stones, the Yardbirds, the Who, David Bowie, Elton John, Pink Floyd, Fleetwood Mac, John Mayall, Long John Baldry and many others cut their teeth at the venue before becoming legends of the music industry. Nigel Planer narrates this documentary exploring the unique experiences of the people who either played at the Island Hotel or went there to listen to music and dance on its famous bouncing dance floor. Contributors include Rod Stewart, Steve Hackett and Dave Brock(n)

programme.title: Sgt Pepper's Musical Revolution - with Howard Goodall
programme.desc: The composer explores why the Beatles' 50-year-old album is still revered as an innovative, revolutionary and influential release. With the help of out-takes, studio conversations between the band and never-heard-before outside of Abbey Road, Howard gets 'under the bonnet' of the album, taking the music apart and reassembling it to reveal how it works. Producer George Martin and his team constructed the album sound by sound, layer by layer - a formula that became the norm for just about every rock act who followed(n)

programme.title: Sinatra: All or Nothing at All
programme.desc: Examination examination of the life, music and career of legendary entertainer Frank Sinatra, told in his own words in archived interviews, along with commentary from those people closest to him. This episode focuses on Sinatra's birth to his early years as a roadhouse performer, revealing the influences behind his meteoric rise. Introduced by Matt Goss(n)

They learn that Ilulissat means iceberg and that an iceberg is a huge chunk of ice which forms on land and then breaks off into the sea. Just then, Glitch's heavy iceberg falls from Grim HQ and starts sliding down the hill towards the town. The Go Jetters need to stop it and protect the town!(n)

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: The team searches for Inkling's long-lost cousin, the elusive Giant Squid(n)

programme.title: Biggleton
programme.desc: In the baker's, Aleem, Frankie and Liam search for a phone, and the Business Woman sits down on some very sticky buns(n)

programme.title: Justin's House
programme.desc: Justin has spent days watching his entire film collection and now decided that he wants to make his own serious action film(n)

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Mo is eager to get a new picture of Tee for her wall, but Tee is more interested in pictures of bugs than posing for the camera himself. Is there any way to do both?(n)

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The sheep creates an imaginary friend when Yabba will not play with him(n)

programme.title: Bing

programme.title: Twirlywoos

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Babies and their grown-ups play with bowls and enjoy songs and story time together(n)

programme.desc: Stacey Dooley experiences the pressure of life on the frontline of mental health services, spending three weeks working alongside staff at Springfield Hospital in London. Villanelle is forced to go on the run, desperate to avoid the wrath of her former employers and plotting to follow her enemy back to London(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce chairs the topical debate from Shrewsbury, where the panel discuss the coronavirus and the implications that are affecting the UK and the rest of the world(n)

programme.title: Noughts + Crosses
programme.desc: Albion is reeling from the aftermath of events at Demwa Hospital, but Sephy has just one question on her mind - how did Callum know what was going to happen? As soon as Ryan discovers what has happened, he gives himself up to the police and confesses in order to save his sons. Meggie is horrified to discover what has happened to her family, and Jasmine offers to pay for Ryan's legal defence costs. Drama, starring Masali Baduza, Jack Rowan, Helen Baxendale and Paterson Joseph(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Part two of two. Danny takes Gerry on the run as they search for the proof they need to prove his innocence - despite Gerry remaining tight-lipped about his involvement with dodgy dealings in his old unit. The stakes are high as the Unsolved Crime and Open Case Squad goes after one of the biggest crime families in London, and it's those closest to Gerry who are dangerously put in the firing line. Starring Dennis Waterman, Nicholas Lyndhurst, Tamzin Outhwaite and Denis Lawson(n)

programme.title: Newscast

programme.title: London Kills

programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of property developments in Plymouth, Treherbert in Wales and Hinckley in Leicestershire that all turn out to take much longer than planned, threatening to prevent their new owners making money(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: When flooding hits a business, the owner goes to extraordinary lengths to try to rubber stamp her claim. A dog owner displays very bad behaviour when it comes to their pet's insurance policy, while there's a backlash over a whiplash case when footage emerges of the claimant in very fine health indeed. Narrated by Naga Munchetty(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams at the Peterborough Festival of Antiques find themselves in the capable hands of experts Stephanie Connell and Philip Serrell, trying to bag items they can make a profit on at auction. As well as monitoring proceedings, presenter Eric Knowles has an intriguing mystery to solve(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Julia supports Jimmi as he struggles with life after prison. Later, she unexpectedly offers him a way out of Letherbridge, but whether he will accept it remains to be seen(n)

programme.title: London Kills
programme.desc: A manipulative murderer strikes too close to home for the squad's comfort. With Billie stabbed and her attacker on the loose, Vivienne blames herself for not recognising that she was in danger. As the team scrambles to track down Amber, they discover that there's more to her than meets the eye. Detective drama, starring Bailey Patrick, Tori Allen-Martin, Sharon Small and Hugo Speer(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple fulfil a long-held dream of moving to the Scottish countryside of Ayrshire. Alongside a selection of the region's best properties that a budget of £450,000 can buy, Nicki also pays a visit to the 18th-century Dumfries House, home to one of the finest collections of furniture in the world(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore searches for tired and unwanted items at Woking Recycling Centre in Surrey she can transform from skip-bound to stylish. With three items saved, Sarah turns to upholsterer Sharon O'Connor to overhaul an old chair frame, while upcycler Jacqui Joseph attempts to transform a dated desk. Sarah's challenge is to update a set of four chairs. Narrated by Arthur Smith(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Siblings from Wolverhampton are paired with chefs Ellis Barrie and Romy Gill. Sommer and Daryl's large family gatherings revolve around shared meals and they were both taught to cook by their grandmother, who cheers them on from the audience. Fiercely competitive, Sommer intends to beat her little brother, but Daryl is determined he can get the better of his sister. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway treks from Harlech to Barmouth, stripping down to basics as he crosses a nudist beach in Gwynedd. On this walk along one of Wales' most popular coastlines, Derek also rolls a cheese down the world's steepest hill and learns about the 'Devil in the Shawl' when he visits an old village chapel. Along his journey, he nearly gets caught out on a tidal causeway and meets a team of women who give up their free time to keep the beaches clean and safe for wildlife(n)

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Gary decides to join the dating scene so he can bring someone along to Stevie's birthday party, prompting Miranda to do the same. Are the two of them destined to find romance in the arms of strangers - or with each other? Meanwhile, the shop owner can't hide her embarrassment when Penny decides to run for local councillor. Comedy, starring Miranda Hart, Tom Ellis and Patricia Hodge(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Ex-Manchester United defender Gary Pallister, former WBC World Super-Middleweight champion Richie Woodhall, Olympic gold medal-winning hockey player Jane Sixsmith, and former sprinter Tony Jarrett join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Six heat winners return for the quarter-final, fighting for a place in Knockout Week. Facing the Critic's Test, the week's best-performing amateurs take on an intimidating task - cooking to a brief set by food writer Jimi Famurewa, who asks for a dish inspired by flavours of the sweet shop. Presented by John Torode and Gregg Wallace(n)

programme.title: Peter Kay's Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: A further collection of clips documenting the much-loved comedy star's best work from his decades in showbusiness. Featuring a special appearance by The One Show's Alex Jones(n)

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian joins the ranks of an airline cabin crew, a job that requires service with a smile - something that simply does not come naturally to Rhod. How will the long-haul passengers cope with his elbow-crunching trolley driving and miserable approach to customer service?(n)

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Pretty Minted
programme.desc: In west Wales 19-year-old forex trader Ash persuades YouTube star Gonth to join him in a new business venture. Utilising Ash's knowledge and Gonth's 1.7million fans, they launching a subscription-only webinar offering advice on how to dabble in the forex market. Elsewhere, Twitch streamer Rage Darling wows her fans at Ragecon and world champion Dan Huxtable opens Wales' largest kickboxing club(n)

programme.title: Witness (1985)
programme.desc: A Philadelphia cop is assigned to protect an Amish boy who was the only witness to a murder. Discovering the killers are rogue policemen willing to go to any lengths to cover up their crime, he is forced to go into hiding within the Amish community, experiencing a clash of cultures and unexpected feelings for the boy's widowed mother. Thriller, directed by Peter Weir, with Harrison Ford, Kelly McGillis, Lukas Haas and Danny Glover(n)

programme.desc: Fiona Bruce chairs the topical debate from Shrewsbury, where the panel discuss the coronavirus and the implications that are affecting the UK and the rest of the world(n)

programme.title: Noughts + Crosses
programme.desc: Albion is reeling from the aftermath of events at Demwa Hospital, but Sephy has just one question on her mind - how did Callum know what was going to happen? As soon as Ryan discovers what has happened, he gives himself up to the police and confesses in order to save his sons. Meggie is horrified to discover what has happened to her family, and Jasmine offers to pay for Ryan's legal defence costs. Drama, starring Masali Baduza, Jack Rowan, Helen Baxendale and Paterson Joseph(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Part two of two. Danny takes Gerry on the run as they search for the proof they need to prove his innocence - despite Gerry remaining tight-lipped about his involvement with dodgy dealings in his old unit. The stakes are high as the Unsolved Crime and Open Case Squad goes after one of the biggest crime families in London, and it's those closest to Gerry who are dangerously put in the firing line. Starring Dennis Waterman, Nicholas Lyndhurst, Tamzin Outhwaite and Denis Lawson(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: When flooding hits a business, the owner goes to extraordinary lengths to try to rubber stamp her claim. programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams at the Peterborough Festival of Antiques find themselves in the capable hands of experts Stephanie Connell and Philip Serrell, trying to bag items they can make a profit on at auction. programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Julia supports Jimmi as he struggles with life after prison. Later, she unexpectedly offers him a way out of Letherbridge, but whether he will accept it remains to be seen(n)

programme.title: London Kills
programme.desc: A manipulative murderer strikes too close to home for the squad's comfort. With Billie stabbed and her attacker on the loose, Vivienne blames herself for not recognising that she was in danger. As the team scrambles to track down Amber, they discover that there's more to her than meets the eye. Detective drama, starring Bailey Patrick, Tori Allen-Martin, Sharon Small and Hugo Speer(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple fulfil a long-held dream of moving to the Scottish countryside of Ayrshire. Alongside a selection of the region's best properties that a budget of £450,000 can buy, Nicki also pays a visit to the 18th-century Dumfries House, home to one of the finest collections of furniture in the world(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore searches for tired and unwanted items at Woking Recycling Centre in Surrey she can transform from skip-bound to stylish. With three items saved, Sarah turns to upholsterer Sharon O'Connor to overhaul an old chair frame, while upcycler Jacqui Joseph attempts to transform a dated desk. Sarah's challenge is to update a set of four chairs. Narrated by Arthur Smith(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Siblings from Wolverhampton are paired with chefs Ellis Barrie and Romy Gill. Sommer and Daryl's large family gatherings revolve around shared meals and they were both taught to cook by their grandmother, who cheers them on from the audience. Fiercely competitive, Sommer intends to beat her little brother, but Daryl is determined he can get the better of his sister. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Ex-Manchester United defender Gary Pallister, former WBC World Super-Middleweight champion Richie Woodhall, Olympic gold medal-winning hockey player Jane Sixsmith, and former sprinter Tony Jarrett join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Gary decides to join the dating scene so he can bring someone along to Stevie's birthday party, prompting Miranda to do the same. Are the two of them destined to find romance in the arms of strangers - or with each other? Meanwhile, the shop owner can't hide her embarrassment when Penny decides to run for local councillor. Comedy, starring Miranda Hart, Tom Ellis and Patricia Hodge(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Actress Olivia Colman, JLS member Aston Merrygold, comedian Jon Richardson and wildlife presenter Michaela Strachan join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Six heat winners return for the quarter-final, fighting for a place in Knockout Week. Presented by John Torode and Gregg Wallace(n)

programme.title: Peter Kay's Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: A further collection of clips documenting the much-loved comedy star's best work from his decades in showbusiness. Featuring a special appearance by The One Show's Alex Jones(n)

programme.title: Witness (1985)
programme.desc: A Philadelphia cop is assigned to protect an Amish boy who was the only witness to a murder. Discovering the killers are rogue policemen willing to go to any lengths to cover up their crime, he is forced to go into hiding within the Amish community, experiencing a clash of cultures and unexpected feelings for the boy's widowed mother. Thriller, directed by Peter Weir, with Harrison Ford, Kelly McGillis, Lukas Haas and Danny Glover(n)

programme.desc: Infidelity leads to a man being found dead in bed and a woman being menaced. Starring Hugo Speer, Sharon Small, Bailey Patrick and Tori Allen-Martin(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to Suffolk to help a couple looking to set down roots in the countryside with a budget of £900,000, and he also meets a dynamic dairy farming family(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore scours the skips at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey for items to restore and sell for a profit. Items include a pair of leather wallets and a rocking chair(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Mother and son duo Pat and Tommy compete in the culinary contest, teaming up with chefs Romy Gill and Ellis Barrie. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Coronavirus Daily Update: BBC News Special
programme.desc: The latest reports about Covid-19. Presented by Rachel Burden(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Geoff Clark from Sunderland hopes that audio expert Mark Stuckey can restore a beloved 1960s jukebox, and horologist Steve Fletcher turns his attention to a 400-year-old wall clock that has laid broken in a box for years. Elsewhere, Tim Gunn restores a stowaway bike to its former glory(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace preside as the last group of six hopefuls compete in the culinary contest. They are challenged to serve up their signature dish, with the judges' favourite two winning a coveted MasterChef apron and a place in the next round. The others must come up with a dish using pigeon, beetroot and bacon in the Three Ingredients Challenge, after which two cooks are sent home, leaving the remaining four to cook two courses for three former contestants(n)

programme.title: Stacey Dooley Investigates
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley experiences the pressure of life on the frontline of mental health services, spending three weeks working alongside staff at Springfield Hospital in London. During her investigation, she sees first hand how tough it is for medical staff to help young people who are struggling with their mental health, and finds out how hard it is make decisions on what is best for them when their lives are on the line(n)

programme.title: Killing Eve
programme.desc: The second series of the spy thriller picks up where the first left off, with MI5 agent Eve and assassin Villanelle left reeling from their final devastating encounter. Eve finds herself summoned back to London by Carolyn, who offers her a new opportunity. Villanelle is forced to go on the run, desperate to avoid the wrath of her former employers and plotting to follow her enemy back to London(n)

programme.title: Killing Eve
programme.desc: Unable to risk going to hospital, Villanelle needs to find someone to take care of her while she recovers, so tricks an unassuming man into letting her recuperate at his house. Meanwhile, Eve, having been taken back under Carolyn's wing, meets her new team and has to tell them everything she has learned about Villanelle - ommitting her own role in the assassin's current situation. Julian Barratt (The Mighty Boosh) guest stars(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: David is devastated about Shona's divorce request but claims it will give him a chance to move on - and when he finds Alina alone in Victoria Garden, it is clear there is a spark between them. News of a family wedding gives Alya the chance she needs to get Yasmeen away from Geoff. The only problem is, they are not invited. Bernie forces Gemma to seek medical help, Evelyn summons Claudia for another makeover and Charles is outraged to discover Ken has smuggled Eccles the dog into his apartment(n)

programme.title: Kate & Koji
programme.desc: Six-part series following Kate, a working-class woman who runs an old-fashioned cafe in a seaside town, as she develops a strong, if sometimes volatile, friendship with an asylum-seeking African doctor, Koji. With Kate and Koji's undercover clinic in full swing, the last thing they need is a visit from Dr Radwan. Medium keeps Koji calm, and Kate cooks up a surefire way to get back at an adversary. Comedy, starring Brenda Blethyn, Jimmy Akingbola, Blake Harrison, Barbara Flynn and Victor McGuire(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: An argument in the Rovers turns to blows when David accuses Seb of being jealous of his friendship with Alina - and when Emma realises her boyfriend still has feeling for his ex, she tells him to find somewhere else to stay. Gemma plays down her depression and assures the doctor she is just exhausted, leaving Bernie frustrated by her daughter's refusal to accept help. Alya and Ryan tell Yasmeen they have been invited to Zeedan's wedding in Spain. Now all they have to do is tell Geoff, who is not on the guest list(n)

programme.title: The Trouble with Maggie Cole
programme.desc: Maggie is horrified that her comments about Roxanna have been interpreted by her Polish father Emil as racist, and she is relieved when they understand what she actually did say. Peter has to contend with a campaign for his resignation, as journalists try to dig up his family skeletons. programme.desc: Joe prepares to take up his new post in London, but secretly regrets the decision to leave the Yorkshire Moors. The youngster lives with his mother and stepfather, but recently his biological father has been back in touch, trying to see the boy - something the courts will not allow. However, tragedy strikes when conflict grows between the angry father and stepfather - with one of the Aidensfield bobbies caught in the crossfire. Rupert Ward-Lewis joins the cast(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective sets out for Norfolk when a country publican begs him to help solve a murder - but is disappointed when the innkeeper turns out to be a budding author and the killing fictional. However, he soon has food for thought when a real death occurs - committed by a seemingly ghostly hand. Geraldine Alexander and Ian McCulloch guest star, with David Suchet(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A philandering husband is found dying by the swimming pool after being shot during a house party at the home of Lady Lucy Angkatell. Legendary Belgian Sleuth Poirot, who is guest of honour at the gathering, appears to have an open-and-shut case on his hands - but the investigator finds himself up against one of his finest adversaries. Whodunit, guest-starring Sarah Miles (Ryan's Daughter), with David Suchet, Lysette Anthony and Edward Fox(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Syndicate 9 find themselves dealing with a high-profile historical case after Mandy Sweeting's body is recovered from a quarry, 23 years after she was first reported missing. However, Rachel's biggest challenge might not be dealing with the media attention or even re-interviewing bereft family members, but hiding her contempt for Rob Waddington's father Frankie (former Coronation Street star Ian Mercer), who led the original investigation. Meanwhile, Janet also has problems to deal with at home, as Elise moves in with her dad and his new girlfriend. But at least it inspires the detective to look at her own love life, as she confides in Rachel that she's started using dating sites. Crime drama, starring Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp and Danny Miller(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A local gigolo is murdered, forcing Jack to face the uncomfortable fact that some women are willing to pay for sex - and triggering an investigation that takes him into the seedy world of male escorts and their clientele. David Jason stars as the old-fashioned copper, with Isla Blair, Russell Hunter, Joanna David and Martin Jarvis(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Gina struggles to cope with Phil's death and refuses Carol's offer to prescribe her sleeping pills, determined to deal with things on her own. However, with the funeral scheduled for the next day, an emotional service that sees the whole of Aidensfield turning out to say their farewells to the much-loved bobby, it seems the grieving landlady is expecting too much of herself. Over at the station, Don is racked with guilt over the shooting of his colleague, convinced he should have taken the bullet. Despite George's reassurances, the bobby believes the villagers will blame him for the death of a close friend and decides to pack his bags - until the sergeant makes him a proposition. Meanwhile, the officers investigate the suspected suicide of a local teacher and Carol fails to report an elderly motorist involved in a hit-and-run with a cyclist, which proves particularly embarrassing when the victim turns out to be the hospital's new doctor. Leon Ockenden joins the cast(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack returns home with Annie and Amos, Frank fusses over his grandson, and Biff announces he cannot be friends with Luke as long as he is with Tina(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack hurts his back and cannot attend Joe's funeral. Eric tries to worm his way into Caroline's good books, and Dave tells Kathy that he loves her and wants them to live together(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Reg is disappointed when Maureen does not buy him a birthday present. Jack tries to get Rodney to employ him as a singer in the bar, and Sean is arrested on suspicion of robbery(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des refuses to be Steve's witness against Sean. Audrey tells Alma about Martin's fling, and Steve blackmails Sean into accepting paying off his debt at £20 a week(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby's former CID colleague Jack Colby left the force under a cloud following a sex scandal involving villagers and officers at Causton police station. Now retrained as a probation officer, Jack is working at a halfway house for reformed criminals, so when he is bludgeoned to death the detective sets out to discover why the past would not leave him alone. With Chris Barrie, Edward Petherbridge, Sue Jenkins, Nigel Harrison and Christopher Fairbank(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Gina struggles to cope with Phil's death and refuses Carol's offer to prescribe her sleeping pills, determined to deal with things on her own. Leon Ockenden joins the cast(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Beautiful Russian aristocrat Countess Vera Rossakoff, the chief suspect in a series of jewel thefts, seemingly captivates Poirot with her charm and intellect, meaning Hastings and Miss Lemon have to do most of the actual detective work and ensure she is brought to justice. Drama, guest starring Kika Markham(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The last run of the detective drama ended with Lewis (Kevin Whately) starting a new life away from the force, but it seems that peace and quiet doesn't suit him - which is just as well, as newly promoted DI Hathaway (Laurence Fox) is struggling to find a sidekick, and within just four weeks is already on to his second sergeant, DS Lizzie Maddox (Angela Griffin). So, Chief Superintendent Innocent (Rebecca Front) decides to reunite the former partners to look into the murder of a neurosurgeon, a case with potential links to the worlds of animal rights and blood sports. Suspicion falls on glamorous widow Erica (Kara Tointon), but it turns out there are plenty of other people in the victim's inner circle who were driven by fear and loathing(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel promised the chaos of her personal life was all in the past, but she is conflicted upon discovering her mother's new boyfriend is a violent domestic abuser. Meanwhile, Janet gets cold feet when she goes on her first date, terrified at the idea of having to impress a stranger. The crimebusters are united in the search for the killer of Rich Hutchings, who is found murdered in the flat he shares with his husband Adam. Barry, their outspoken and violent homophobic neighbour, is the prime suspect. However, as Adam's friend Tim struggles to recall the events of their night out, it seems Barry may be in the clear. Meanwhile, Rachel is horrified when her mum Sharon turns up asking for help. Crime drama, starring Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp and Danny Miller(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The discovery of a woman's body in a car park presents Jack Frost with another challenge to his deductive powers. Delving into the victim's electronic organiser, he learns she was connected with a local religious community - and realises someone in the congregation is more interested in the pleasures of this world than the rewards of the next. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander and John Lyons(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A student is attacked on campus, and Jack takes time off from questioning two robbery suspects to catch the thug responsible before he can strike again. The trail leads him to Boxborough - where a confrontation with a belligerent solicitor provides the clue he needs to crack the case. Starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons, Charlotte Bellamy and Tazmin Malleson(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Robin borrow Mr Roper's car to drive to Southampton for a football match. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan and Paula Wilcox(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Lesley Ashton (former Emmerdale actor Emma Atkins) arrives in Aidensfield looking for Don, and it is quickly clear that he is less than happy to see her, but despite his cool welcome she seems determined to make a new life for herself in the village. Elsewhere, Gina is pleased to be offered accommodation at the pub, Bernie receives a surprising offer from the recently widowed Iris Grocott and Ralph Spurling seeks Oscar's services as a private investigator after his house is broken into. Dominic Jephcott and Ann Mitchell guest star(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dave tells Biff that he does not know where he stands with Kathy. Sarah is worried about how Jack and Annie are coping with Joe's death(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Terry worries that Britt is having a breakdown. Zak announces his new plans for Dingle Enterprises, and Lady Weir tells Alan that Danny is her son(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Martin does not know where he stands with Gail. Rodney hosts a karaoke night at the Rovers, and Mike is shocked when he discovers his car is registered as stolen(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bet is flattered when Rodney calls on her with flowers, Mike puts his car up for sale, and Josie is stunned by Don's confession about the phone calls he made to Denise(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby becomes an unwelcome guest at baron Ned Fitzroy's wedding to Beth Porteous when the maid of honour is found pinned to the wall with a dagger. Meanwhile, Cully gets cold feet about tying the knot with Simon and is disappointed to learn the honeymoon to New Zealand will have to be cut short. With his own daughter's nuptials to attend, Barnaby races to unearth the family secrets he is sure lie behind the Fitzroy tragedy. Detective drama, starring John Nettles and Laura Howard(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Lesley Ashton (former Emmerdale actor Emma Atkins) arrives in Aidensfield looking for Don, and it is quickly clear that he is less than happy to see her, but despite his cool welcome she seems determined to make a new life for herself in the village. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A lavish dinner party proves to be a prelude to murder and intrigue - but fortunately Poirot is among the guests, and soon realises the key to the mystery lies in a duel fought 10 years previously. Drama, starring David Suchet, Caroline Langrishe and John McEnery(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Drama series, starring Martin Clunes as a surgeon who takes up a post in a sleepy Cornish village. Martin has to rush Buddy the dog to the vets. Meanwhile, Mrs Tishell's young cousin Sarah joins her for some work experience, but it does not go to plan, and one of Louisa's clients arrives at the surgery unannounced, posing a dilemma(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: In the final episode of this series, Martin rushes to Ruth's for a medical emergency, and preparations for Morwenna and Al's wedding do not go to plan. Elsewhere, Martin takes his final GMC assessment under the watchful eye of eminent surgeon Bernard Newton, who monitors Martin's skills in assisted surgery. Starring Martin Clunes, Tom Conti, Caroline Catz and Jessica Ransom(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: As Syndicate 9 investigates the discovery of a woman's body in a Manchester hotel room, Gill introduces Rachel to high-flying DS Will Pemberton, unaware they are already well acquainted - they've been having a passionate, secret relationship for months. But while the volatile copper can be discreet when it comes to romance, will she be able to hide her feelings when Gill lets slip that she wasn't the first choice for the sergeant's job? Meanwhile, Janet is keen to give her own love life a boost, especially after seeing how happy Ade is with his new partner Eleanor. However, a speed-dating event might not be the best option, as Janet finds herself treating each brief encounter like a police interrogation. Crime drama, starring Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp, Amelia Bullmore, Steve Touissant and Mina Anwar(n)
